svg.text: Memory system — how Sage gets better every day
Three skills capture knowledge. One MCP layer persists it all.

g.text: Captures mistakes as structured prevention rules
Example: stored after a user correction
WHEN: Touching the billing API webhook handler
CHECK: Webhook signature is verified before processing
BECAUSE: Unverified webhooks caused duplicate charges on Mar 12
Stores project knowledge as focused prose insights — one per entry, tagged by domain
Example: stored after /learn scanned the auth module
Title: Auth uses short-lived JWTs with refresh token rotation
Content: Access tokens expire in 15 min. Refresh tokens are single-use
and rotated on every refresh. Token blacklist in Redis handles
revocation. /auth/refresh is the critical path — if slow, every
Maps entity relationships and dependencies — what depends on what
Example: stored after /learn mapped the billing subsystem
Depends on: payments (Stripe SDK), users (account ownership)
Insight: Changing billing affects 3 downstream services.
Webhook handler must update in sync with billing schema changes.
Persistent, project-scoped. Survives across sessions and agents.

svg.text: How stored knowledge improves every session
Corrections, knowledge, and relationships accumulate across every session
better work → fewer mistakes → but captured when they happen → even better next time
The agent that failed you on Monday doesn't repeat it on Tuesday.
